# services.lubelogger

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.lubelogger`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.lubelogger.dataDir` | `string` | Path to LubeLogger config and metadata inside of `/var/lib/`. |
| `services.lubelogger.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable LubeLogger, a self-hosted, open-source, web-based vehicle maintenance and fuel milage tracker. |
| `services.lubelogger.environmentFile` | `null or absolute path` | Path to a file containing extra LubeLogger config options in the systemd `EnvironmentFile` format. Refer to the [documentation] for supported options. \[documentation\]: https://docs.lubelogger.com/Advanced/Environment%20Variables This can be used to pass secrets to LubeLogger without putting them in the Nix store. For example, to set an SMTP password, point `environmentFile` at a file containing: `MailConfig__Password=<pass>` |
| `services.lubelogger.group` | `string` | Group under which LubeLogger runs. |
| `services.lubelogger.openFirewall` | `boolean` | Open ports in the firewall for the LubeLogger web interface. |
| `services.lubelogger.package` | `package` | The lubelogger package to use. |
| `services.lubelogger.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | The TCP port LubeLogger will listen on. |
| `services.lubelogger.settings` | `attribute set of string` | Additional configuration for LubeLogger, see <https://docs.lubelogger.com/Environment%20Variables> for supported values. |
| `services.lubelogger.user` | `string` | User account under which LubeLogger runs. |
